pubmed_links: [Huang et al. (2019) TWAS and mRNA expression identifies ASD candidate genes including MED15](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30561910/);[De Luca et al. (2003) CAG repeats in PCQAP/MED15 associated with schizophrenia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/12497610/);[Woodward et al. (2019) 22q11.2 duplications with enriched MED15 brain expression linked to ASD](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30614210/)
summary: MED15 (also known as PCQAP), a Mediator transcriptional coactivator subunit located in the 22q11.2 DiGeorge syndrome region, has been implicated in both autism spectrum disorder and schizophrenia; it was identified as one of five validated candidate genes in an integrative transcriptome-wide association and mRNA expression study of ASD, and CAG repeat variations in the gene showed initial association with schizophrenia susceptibility. (emerging evidence)
